name: contest-mcp
description: Log amateur-radio QSOs to N3FJP logging software from Claude.
long_description: Connects to the N3FJP logging software's TCP API (shared across Amateur Contact Log and the 100+ N3FJP contest loggers) and exposes it as a small set of logically-grouped tools. Log contacts with the automatic Call → CALLTAB → exchange → ENTER flow, read and search the log, run dupe and band/mode operations, and reach anything else through an escape hatch. Safe by design: reads are read-only, all writes (including adding, editing, or deleting individual records) sit at the approval tier so you can allow them globally for automation, and only whole-database wipes are hard-blocked unless a dedicated off-by-default switch is enabled. Verified live against N3FJP API version 2.2. Pairs with fldigi-mcp (which operates the radio) for an end-to-end operate-and-log workflow.

tools.name: status
tools.description: Snapshot: program, version, API version, QSO count, band/mode/frequency.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Read-only program queries: paths, serial, QSO rate, band/mode/frequency.

tools.name: fields
tools.description: Read entry-box values; list visible / all fields.

tools.name: search
tools.description: List recent QSOs, search the log, dupe check, entity status.

tools.name: log
tools.description: Log a QSO: set fields, CALLTAB, ENTER (the automatic-logging flow).

tools.name: bandmode
tools.description: Change band, mode, and frequency before logging.

tools.name: notifications
tools.description: Enable/disable and drain N3FJP push events.

tools.name: database
tools.description: Add-direct, delete a record, or raw SQL (approval tier; whole-DB wipe gated).

tools.name: n3fjp_call
tools.description: Escape hatch: call any N3FJP command by name, incl. future ones.

n3fjp_host.title: N3FJP host
n3fjp_host.description: Host where N3FJP runs. Leave as 127.0.0.1 unless N3FJP is on another computer.

n3fjp_port.title: N3FJP API port
n3fjp_port.description: N3FJP's API port (Settings → Application Program Interface). Default 1100. Note: this is NOT the multi-PC networking port.

n3fjp_timeout.title: Timeout (seconds)
n3fjp_timeout.description: How long to wait for N3FJP to respond.

allow_db_wipe.title: ⚠️ Allow whole-database wipe/overwrite (DANGER)
allow_db_wipe.description: Leave OFF. When OFF, the server refuses any raw SQL that could delete or overwrite your ENTIRE log (DROP/TRUNCATE, or DELETE/UPDATE with no WHERE). Only turn this ON if you fully understand the risk and have backed up your log — it permits irreversible, whole-database operations.
